prompt:
Bug: in the Workouts tab, the FAILED/EASY/GOOD/HARD difficulty toggles are visible at all times on every exercise card. They should only appear after the user has tapped the **Done** button for that exercise (and disappear again if Done is toggled off). **Repro:** - Open the Workouts tab - Look at any exercise card: the four difficulty buttons render below the Done button, even when the exercise is not yet marked done. They should be hidden until the user marks Done. **File:** `src/tabs/Workouts/components/ExerciseCard.tsx` **Current code (lines 221-282)** renders the difficulty grid and the failed-set/failed-rep sub-controls unconditionally: ```tsx
{(['failed', 'easy', 'good', 'hard'] as const).map((d) => { ... })}
{difficulty === 'failed' && (
... failed-set/failed-rep range inputs ...
)} ``` **Fix:** wrap **both** the difficulty grid AND the failed-state sub-block in `{completed && (...)}`. The notes textarea immediately below (lines 284-291) should remain unconditionally visible — only the difficulty controls are gated. `completed` is already in scope (line 28: `const completed = entry?.completed ?? false;`). Suggested shape: ```tsx {completed && ( <>
{(['failed', 'easy', 'good', 'hard'] as const).map((d) => { ... })}
{difficulty === 'failed' && (
... range inputs ...
)} )} ``` (Use Preact's Fragment shorthand `<>...` — confirmed the project is Preact via package.json.) **Verify the toggleDone interaction still works correctly:** - toggleDone (lines 88-103) already handles the case where a user un-completes: `{ remove: !weight && !difficulty && !notes }`. So if a user marks Done → picks 'hard' → un-marks Done, the difficulty is preserved in the log. Re-marking Done should show the toggles again with 'hard' still selected. Confirm this round-trip works in your test. - Line 223 (`const selected = difficulty === d || (!difficulty && d === 'good');`) — the default-to-good behavior remains correct: when completed is first toggled on with no prior difficulty, the grid appears and 'good' shows as selected. Keep this logic unchanged. **Add e2e test coverage.** There are existing Playwright tests under `tests/` (look at `tests/workouts.spec.ts` for the existing pattern). Add a test `difficulty toggles hidden until Done is tapped` that: 1. Renders the Workouts tab with at least one exercise 2. Asserts `[data-testid="difficulty-0-good"]` (and the other three difficulty test IDs for index 0) are not visible / not in the DOM 3. Clicks `[data-testid="done-0"]` 4. Asserts the four difficulty buttons are now visible 5. Clicks done-0 again (un-complete) 6. Asserts the difficulty buttons are hidden again The existing test IDs to use: `done-${index}`, `difficulty-${index}-${failed|easy|good|hard}`. **Validation:** - `nix develop .#test --command npx playwright test --project=chromium` — full e2e suite passes including your new test - `nix develop --command npm run typecheck` — no TS errors - `nix develop --command bash -c "npm ci && npm run build"` — production build succeeds **Out of scope:** do not refactor anything else in ExerciseCard; do not touch the notes textarea visibility; do not change the toggleDone logic. **PR title:** "workouts: hide difficulty toggles until exercise is marked done" **PR body:** brief description of the regression + the gate fix + note that an e2e test was added to prevent recurrence.
